title: Oportun Reports First Quarter 2024 Financial Results
text: Oportun reported improved first-quarter 2024 performance with sharply reduced losses. The company posted a GAAP net loss of $26 million compared to $102 million in the prior year—a $76 million improvement. On an adjusted basis, the company returned to profitability with adjusted net income of $3.6 million versus a $58 million loss in Q1 2023. Total revenue declined 3% year-over-year to $250 million, primarily due to a $218 million decrease in average daily principal balance, partially offset by higher portfolio yields. Operating expenses decreased substantially, falling 25% year-over-year to $110 million. CEO Raul Vazquez stated cost reduction efforts resulted in a 15% sequential and 25% year-over-year decline in operating expenses. Aggregate originations of $338 million (down 17% year-over-year). Portfolio yield of 32.5%, up 113 basis points. 30+ Day delinquency rate of 5.2%, improved from 5.5%. Annualized net charge-off rate of 12.0%, flat versus prior year. Management raised full-year 2024 guidance: Total Revenue: $985-$1,010 million. Adjusted EBITDA: $80-$90 million. Net Charge-Off Rate: 11.9% ±50 basis points. The company reaffirmed expectations to achieve adjusted net income profitability in 2024.
